segments.text: Introduction to the vocabulary and biology of Cancer

Transformation
The failure of cells to remain constrained in their growth properties and give rise to tumors.
transformation
Normal				Cancer

Benign Tumor
A tumor that grows to a certain size and then stops, usually with a fibrous capsule surrounding the mass of cells

Malignant Tumor
A tumor whose cells can invade surrounding tissues and spread to other organs

Metastasis
The spreading of malignant tumor cells throughout the body so that tumors develop at new sites

Chemotherapy Targets
S Phase Cells
Examples:
Ara C-has arabinose instead of a ribose sugar.  The inverted hydoxyl in the nucleotide interferes with chain elongation and slows down DNA synthesis
6 azauracil-inhibits OMP decarboxylase in the process of pyrimidine nucleotide synthesis.  Lack of pyrimidine nucleotides impairs DNA synthesis.
